title: cheilanthes subcordata
intro: Cheilanthes subcordata, also known as the fan fern or umbrella fern, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. Native to the western coast of North America, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. Cheilanthes subcordata contains saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and has also been used in herbal remedies for its antioxidant and antiseptic properties, promoting overall health and well-being.
